title: Cage Supervisor
description:
JOB SCOPE

Responsible for enforcing internal controls and standard operating procedures. Complies with all regulatory reporting requirements and key controls. Duties and responsibilities include but not limited to:

  • Supervise and train Cage Cashiers and Senior Cage Cashiers who process various financial transactions for casino guests.
  • Handle and resolve disputes
  • Recommend procedure changes for operational efficiency
  • Maintain preventative maintenance on cage equipment and calls service as needed
  • Oversee the confidentiality of all gaming records
  • Maintain a dependable attendance record, and meet the guidelines with irregular absences and/or lateness record. Act as role model to subordinates
  • Perform other duties as assigned

Team Members Management

  • Check Cashier transactions as per cage policies and procedures
  • Provide a positive work environment, encourage teamwork, and maintains superior customer service to all internal and external customers
  • Evaluate and counsel direct reports in order to maintain and enhance operations of the Department
  • Determine manpower requirements and approve schedules

Guest service

  • Acknowledge everyone immediately with a smile
  • Make eye contact and greet people
  • Speak first-Speak last
  • Personalize each guest and Team Member contact
  • Ensure excellent guest courtesy and satisfaction
  • Be alert for Guests and other Team Members who need assistance and offer help
  • Work closely with other internal Departments to ensure all gaming Guests' needs are handled correctly and professionally

JOB REQUIREMENTS
Education & Certification

  • Secondary education/Diploma preferred

Experience

  • Minimum of three years experience in casino business at least two year as a cage cashier

Other Prerequisites

  • Able to communicate effectively with both English and Mandarin-speaking guests
  • Provide outstanding guest service by being consistently helpful and courteous to all Guests.
  • Possess a flair for numerical and excellent service and communication skills
  • Experience in handling cash and foreign exchange currencies preferred
  • Able to operate electronic cashier equipment (e.g. note counters, counterfeit detectors)
  • Willing to work various shifts, including mornings, afternoons, and overnight, as well as on public holidays
  • Meet the legal age to enter the Casino for work purposes

Marina Bay Sands is committed to building a diverse, equitable and inclusive workforce, providing equal opportunities as we grow our talent base to match our growth ambitions in Singapore. Our employees are committed to adhere to and abide by all rules, regulations, policies and procedures, including the rules of conduct and business ethics of the Company.

postedCompany.description:
Since its opening in 2010, Marina Bay Sands has stood as an architectural marvel and the crown jewel in Singapore’s skyline. Home to the world’s most spectacular rooftop infinity pool and approximately 1,850 rooms and suites, the integrated resort offers exceptional dining, shopping, meeting and entertainment choices, complete with a year-round calendar of signature events.

Marina Bay Sands is dedicated to being a good corporate citizen to serve its people, communities and environment. It drives social impact through its community engagement programme, Sands Cares, and leads environmental stewardship through its global sustainability programme, Sands ECO360.
